title: The New Sound
notes:
This record is extremely. Extremely what? I still struggle to put it into words. It is chaotic, fun music theory that makes you do a double take every 32 bars. He tactfully tackles a lot of complex emotions and cultural stigma about the stereotypical hyper-masculine man. Whether it reaches that audience and whether they take in the message is another beast. Gordie talked a bit about the album straddling a line between listenable in the background and meant to be listened to as an activity. I personally think it veers on the side of actively listening to the album, otherwise you lose a lot of the musical intricacy.

The musicality of all the songs on this album is one thing that elevates this album for me. Groove switches in *Holy, Holy*, the obsurd riff at the beginning of *Blue*, and other incredible pieces of musicianship are tasteful and unique. Every time I listen, I hear something new.
